title: Agent Control Contract — Metrics Registry (metrics_schema 1.0)
description: DATA catalogue (not a JSON-Schema validator) of the frozen, versioned Prometheus metrics a conformant agent exposes on the opt-in /metrics surface, gated by manifest surfaces.metrics_schema. Metrics are Prometheus 0.0.4 text exposition, not a JSON document, so a per-metric registry — neutral agent_* names with agentd_* reference aliases — is the right artifact for agentctl to codegen dashboards/alerts/scalers from. Versioned additive-within-major (new series / new bounded label values tolerated); removal/rename bumps major. A consumer MUST ignore unknown additive series + unknown bounded-label values and refuse only an unknown major. NOTE: the reference exposition (obs/metrics.rs render()) emits ONLY counters and gauges — NO histograms — so all histograms here are provisional/unbuilt with bucket boundaries undefined.

prefix.cutover: GA (P0) — branded agentd_ spelling is the current alias agents in the field emit; NOT dropped before GA. Consumers MUST accept both until GA.

exposition: Prometheus 0.0.4 text exposition; no metrics/prometheus crate (hand-rendered in obs/metrics.rs render()); /metrics carries NO version label — the version is discovered out-of-band via manifest surfaces.metrics_schema.
cardinality_rule: Bounded labels only. NEVER carry run_id / agent_id / agent_path / call_id / session_id / URI as a label. Closed enum domains each carry an `other` overflow slot. The `server` label is config-time string bounded structurally by a 16-slot intern table (MCP_SERVER_SLOTS); once full, further names fold into server="other".

label_domain_kinds.closed: value is a key of closed_label_domains — a frozen enum ending in `other`
label_domain_kinds.open: free string value (e.g. model) — unbounded by contract, used only where source guarantees low cardinality
label_domain_kinds.open_bounded: config-time string bounded structurally (e.g. server folded to 16 intern slots + `other`)

histograms_note: RFC 0016 §4.3 (agent_run_duration_ms / agent_intel_call_duration_ms / agent_tool_call_duration_ms) and RFC 0010 §3.8 (otel-only agent_tool_duration_ms / agent_loop_step_duration_ms / gen_ai.client.operation.duration) name the duration histograms differently and the reference /metrics exposition emits NONE of them. All histogram entries below are status=provisional with buckets UNDEFINED; consumers MUST treat them as absent unless a future surfaces flag advertises them.
value_type_note: All gauges/counters render as integers EXCEPT agent_saturation, which renders as a float in [0,1] (stored as basis points, divided by 10000). A consumer codegen that assumes a uniform u64 value type breaks on agent_saturation.

metric_record_shape.name: string REQUIRED — neutral agent_* canonical name
metric_record_shape.neutral_name: string REQUIRED — duplicate of name for explicitness (de-brand canonical)
metric_record_shape.type: enum REQUIRED — counter | gauge | histogram (frozen 3-way set)
metric_record_shape.labels: Label[] REQUIRED (may be empty []) — each { key, domain }
metric_record_shape.scaling_role: string OPTIONAL — role within the autoscaling subset
metric_record_shape.aliases_scaling: string[] OPTIONAL — cross-RFC name aliases for the same signal

metrics.name: agent_ready
metrics.help: 1 when ready to accept work (not draining / lame-ducked).
metrics.note: RFC 0015 §5.5 listed standalone agentd_draining + agentd_lame_duck gauges; source emits neither — drain/lame-duck observable via agent_ready==0 + agent_drains_total.

metrics.name: agent_paused
metrics.help: 1 while the agentic tree is paused at turn boundaries (RFC 0015 §4.3). Pause is NOT readiness.
metrics.note: Source-only addition vs RFC 0016 §4.3; owned by RFC 0015.

metrics.name: agent_tokens_total
metrics.note: RFC 0016 specifies labels {model,type}; reference emits ONLY {type} (AgentMsg::Usage lacks model). model label deferred — divergence, source wins.

metrics.name: agent_intel_calls_total
metrics.note: RFC 0016 specifies a {model} label; reference emits it bare. model label deferred.

metrics.name: agent_active_subagents
metrics.help: Subagents currently alive in the tree.

metrics.name: agent_tree_breadth
metrics.help: Current max siblings at any tree node.

metrics.name: agent_intel_up
metrics.help: 1 when the intelligence endpoint is reachable.
metrics.note: intel/health.rs prose names this agentd_intel_endpoint_up; the rendered name is agentd_intel_up. Contract canonicalizes the rendered name.

metrics.name: agent_mcp_connect_failures_total
metrics.note: server label is a config-time string bounded by a 16-slot intern table (MCP_SERVER_SLOTS); overflow folds into server="other".

metrics.name: agent_config_reload_total
metrics.note: Source-only addition vs RFC 0016 §4.3; owned by RFC 0017.

metrics.name: agent_config_generation
metrics.note: Source-only addition vs RFC 0016 §4.3; owned by RFC 0017.

metrics.name: agent_pending_events
metrics.help: Reactive events received but not yet routed.
metrics.note: P10 reconciliation: this source-emitted RFC 0016 name is CANONICAL. RFC 0019 §5.1 agent_reactive_backlog is an ALIAS for the same signal (RFC 0019 admits it is mechanically a sum).

metrics.name: agent_reaction_lag_ms
metrics.help: Age of the oldest un-routed pending event (ms).

metrics.name: agent_saturation
metrics.source: obs/metrics.rs render() (gauge_f64, stored basis points /10000); RFC 0019 §5.1
metrics.note: FLOAT-valued in [0,1] — the only non-integer series; defeats uniform u64 codegen.

metrics.name: agent_claims_lost_total
metrics.help: Work claims lost to another replica (RFC 0019 §5.1).
metrics.scaling_role: over-provision / scale-down signal (high under low backlog ⇒ scale down)

metrics.name: agent_claims_granted_total
metrics.help: Work claims granted to this replica (RFC 0019 §3.2).

metrics.name: agent_claims_released_total
metrics.help: Held claims released back to the fleet (RFC 0019 §3.3/§6).

metrics.name: agent_memory_max_bytes
metrics.note: Emitted only when the kernel exposes the cgroup v2 field; absent otherwise (graceful degradation).

metrics.name: agent_memory_current_bytes
metrics.note: Emitted only when the kernel exposes the cgroup v2 field; absent otherwise.

metrics.name: agent_runs_started_total
metrics.note: Legacy bare series retained additively; prefer agent_runs_total{status} for terminal accounting.

metrics.name: agent_runs_completed_total
metrics.note: Legacy bare series; subsumed by agent_runs_total{status="completed"}.

metrics.name: agent_runs_failed_total
metrics.help: Supervised runs that failed on infra.
metrics.note: Legacy bare series; subsumed by agent_runs_total{status}.

metrics.name: agent_runs_killed_total
metrics.help: Supervised runs torn down by the supervisor.
metrics.note: Legacy bare series.

metrics.name: agent_reactions_total
metrics.note: Legacy bare series retained additively.

metrics.name: agent_tokens_input_total
metrics.help: Input tokens reported by direct children.
metrics.note: Legacy bare series; prefer agent_tokens_total{type="in"}.

metrics.name: agent_tokens_output_total
metrics.help: Output tokens reported by direct children.
metrics.note: Legacy bare series; prefer agent_tokens_total{type="out"}.

metrics.name: agent_restarts_tripped_total
metrics.note: Legacy bare series retained additively.

metrics.name: agent_run_duration_ms
metrics.note: Reference emits NO histograms; bucket boundaries UNDEFINED. RFC 0010 §3.8 names a similar duration as otel-only. Consumers treat as absent unless surfaces advertises.

metrics.name: agent_intel_call_duration_ms
metrics.note: Reference emits NO histograms; buckets UNDEFINED.

metrics.name: agent_tool_call_duration_ms
metrics.source: RFC 0016 §4.3 (NOT emitted; RFC 0010 §3.8 otel-only as agent_tool_duration_ms)
metrics.note: Reference emits NO histograms; buckets UNDEFINED. Name conflicts with RFC 0010 §3.8 agent_tool_duration_ms (otel-only).

metrics.name: agent_mcp_up
metrics.help: PROVISIONAL — 1 when a declared MCP server is connected (RFC 0016 §4.3).
metrics.source: RFC 0016 §4.3 (NOT emitted by reference — no declared-server registration hook)
metrics.note: DIVERGENCE: in RFC 0016 but reference does not emit it. server label would fold to the same 16-slot bound as agent_mcp_connect_failures_total.

metrics.name: agent_tool_calls_total
metrics.source: RFC 0016 §4.3 (NOT emitted by reference registry; logs/otel only)
metrics.note: DIVERGENCE: declared in RFC 0016 but not in the reference render() registry.

metrics.name: agent_tokens_per_sec
metrics.note: P10: RFC 0019 autoscaling name NOT emitted by reference; derived/provisional.

metrics.name: agent_intelligence_latency_ms
metrics.note: P10: RFC 0019 autoscaling name NOT emitted by reference; provisional. See agent_intel_call_duration_ms (histogram, also provisional).
